metrics.narrative: Khansaheb's stated business value is faster supplier onboarding, improved compliance, reduced manual processing, and automated 3-way invoice matching (Salesforce MEDDPICC field, last updated 19 Feb 2026), echoed live on 30 September 2025 when Abraham Mathew asked whether the system could assess contractors' financial strength from uploaded balance sheets ahead of large future construction projects. No dollar-quantified ROI has surfaced in any call read for this sweep, and the ROI workshop that would normally produce one has not happened, so the business case remains directional rather than proven.

champion.narrative: Abraham Mathew, Head of Procurement, is Khansaheb's clear day-to-day champion: he led the 22 July 2025 discovery call, presented internally after the 29 July 2025 demo, sat through both onsite POC sessions (7 and 14 Oct 2025), and personally offered on 18 May 2026 to brief Bilal Hamadeh and arrange a presentation to upper management (potentially with the deal owner's manager). His access to Bilal is real but currently indirect and unconfirmed as delivered, and every re-engagement since October 2025 has been rep-initiated after periods of silence rather than buyer-initiated, so his advocacy is genuine but not yet proven to move the economic buyer.

competition.narrative: Khansaheb confirmed on 16 April 2026 that it has shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one of them (declining to name the other), and reconfirmed the same two-vendor shortlist on 18 May 2026; Salesforce records this only as 'Others.' The existing ERP, SAP S/4HANA (existing ERP, integration point not a competing bid), surfaced repeatedly as an integration point (one-way/two-way sync questions on 30 Oct 2025) but is not a competing bid. No sentiment, pricing comparison, or capability gap versus the unnamed rival has been captured in any source read.

identify_pain.narrative: Khansaheb's core pain is manual, paper-based vendor onboarding (KYC/AML/compliance documents) across multiple legal entities, plus manual invoice processing with no real-time supplier visibility (Salesforce MEDDPICC field). This was corroborated live: on 30 September 2025 Abraham Mathew asked for a way to assess contractors' financial strength from uploaded balance sheets ahead of future construction projects, and on 30 October 2025 a call participant described the property management and development team's 'frequent interruptions and failures due to direct integration with the government portal.'

sources: Absence confirmed across Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, and all read Avoma calls
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, order form, or any contracting artifact appears in the Next Step log, Next Step History, or any Avoma call read for this sweep; the deal has not progressed past pricing/commercial scoping (last discussed 30 Oct 2025). This is normal for a Shortlisted-stage deal, but it means there is no forcing document anchoring the close date, and the current spend freeze gives Khansaheb no reason to start one.

economic_buyer.narrative: Bilal Hamadeh is named as economic buyer in Salesforce MEDDPICC (last updated 19 Feb 2026) and in the Next Step log, described as the GM who must take the pilot to management for approval, with Abraham Mathew coordinating the procurement side. Bilal was demoed to directly on 7 August 2025 ('Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged') and joined the 13-14 October 2025 onsite POC. He has had no confirmed direct Zycus conversation since: at the most recent 18 May 2026 meeting, Abraham noted 'Bilal the EB was unavailable' and said he would raise it with Bilal on Zycus's behalf. Access to power is therefore identified but currently dormant.

decision_process.narrative: The process ran from discovery (22 Jul 2025) through a demo to Abraham (29 Jul 2025, entering Formal Evaluation), an onsite POC and platform demo (7 and 14 Oct 2025), then commercial scoping (30 Oct 2025). On 30 October 2025 the process itself changed: Khansaheb shifted from a group-wide deployment to a single-business-unit pilot that must go to Bilal Hamadeh and then to management for approval, with no independent budget authority at Abraham's level. Since then the process has stalled under the regional conflict/blockade, with Khansaheb saying only 'management are evaluating' (20 Dec 2025) and that the project is 'still on and under review with our consultant' (17 Feb 2026) - no firm decision date exists.

sources: Avoma call 'POC Plan to be review and confirm', 2025-09-30
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria were laid out in Salesforce MEDDPICC (self-registration/KYC-AML vendor portal, 3-way invoice matching, multi-ERP integration, process automation, compliance/document validation, cost efficiency) and demonstrated point-by-point on 30 September and 14 October 2025, when the Zycus solutions consultant walked through prequalification scoring, document-expiry alerts, and AP Smartdesk OCR matching against Khansaheb's own registration-form documents. On 14 October 2025 the initial rollout scope was confirmed as supplier onboarding, supplier relationship management, eInvoicing, Merlin intake/insights, and strategic sourcing.

The new RVP introduced himself directly to Abraham, acknowledged the external challenges that had paused the deal, and put the ball in Khansaheb's court to resume talks when ready — so the deal now sits waiting on Abraham to respond.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A small ($150,000), partner-led pilot with proven product fit at the working level but no closed process: it is capped at Shortlisted, the economic buyer has been disengaged for roughly nine months, and the close timeline now depends entirely on a regional conflict/blockade spend freeze with no confirmed resumption date.

deal_risk.text: Khansaheb has paused all non-essential spend, including new construction, due to a regional conflict/blockade, with no resumption date given as of 18 May 2026, directly threatening the 31 August 2026 close date.
Deal size has already been trimmed once (30 Oct 2025) to stay above viable threshold; a further reduction on resumption could put the $150,000 amount at risk again.
No paper process has ever been discussed, so even a resumed pilot would need a full contracting cycle within the days remaining to the recorded close date.

win_position.text: Khansaheb's champion, Abraham Mathew, reconfirmed on 18 May 2026 that Zycus remains one of only two shortlisted vendors, a position that has held since at least 16 April 2026.

Product fit is proven at the working level: two onsite POC/demo sessions (7 and 14 Oct 2025) covering supplier onboarding, eInvoicing, and Merlin intake landed well, and the GM (economic buyer) was 'very engaged' in the 7 August 2025 demo.

The economic buyer has had no confirmed direct Zycus conversation since the 14 October 2025 onsite, including the 18 May 2026 meeting where he was again unavailable, leaving Abraham Mathew as the sole active thread.

The deal has been narrowed to a single-business-unit pilot that Khansaheb itself called 'on the cusp of being too small' on 30 October 2025, at a stage that caps confidence regardless of relationship warmth.

deal_momentum.text: The only substantive engagement in the last 90 days is the single 18 May 2026 check-in; every re-engagement since October 2025 (13 Mar, 25 Mar, 16 Apr, 18 May 2026) followed a period of buyer silence or out-of-office responses and was rep-initiated.
No future meeting, milestone, or dated next step exists beyond a vague 'later in the year' spend-freeze resumption.

Khansaheb has repeatedly reaffirmed, even while paused, that the project 'is still on' (17 Feb 2026) and that Zycus remains shortlisted (16 Apr and 18 May 2026), so the deal has not been explicitly killed.

customer_commitment.text: Abraham Mathew has stayed engaged as champion for nearly a year, providing sample documents and platform feedback, and offered on 18 May 2026 to personally raise the business case with the economic buyer and with upper management.
Beyond Abraham Mathew, no other Khansaheb stakeholder has engaged directly with Zycus since October 2025.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 16 Apr 2026 rep note from Dan Quinn: 'Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one... current expectation is that they will resume in Q2.' Per 17 Feb 2026: 'Not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments".' Abraham Mathew is shortlisting Zycus but is not engaged on any AI conversation.
• Per the 19 Nov 2025 rep note: 'As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our "min prepared to support", still a small deal but could be acceptable.' Product scope is iSource;iSupplier;eInvoicing;iRisk - foundational only, no Merlin/Agentic/ANA. Avoma meetings (9 total, khansaheb.com domain) focus on Crowe-led RFP mechanics, not AI.
• High confidence: 9 valid Avoma meetings on Khansaheb domain, products in scope have no AI component, BD = Unengaged, project on pause due to war. Multiple rep notes spanning Nov 2025-Apr 2026 contain zero references to Merlin/Agentic/AI from any named Khansaheb stakeholder. Clear AI Resistant.
hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: "Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes."<span style="font-family: Tahoma,sans-serif; font-size: 11pt;"> appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business, reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo.... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20
